perturbation.original_text: The term "Information", as used in this Agreement, means all specifications, drawings, sketches, models, samples, reports, plans, forecasts, current or historical data, computer programs or documentation and all other technical, financial or business data, as well as any trade secrets or other information concerning the business, customers, methods, operations and services of a party.
perturbation.changed_text: The term "Information", as used in this Agreement, means only the specifications and drawings explicitly marked as confidential. All other data, including sketches, models, samples, reports, plans, forecasts, current or historical data, computer programs or documentation and all other technical, financial or business data, as well as any trade secrets or other information concerning the business, customers, methods, operations and services of a party, are not considered Information under this agreement.
perturbation.explanation: This change narrows the definition of 'Information' to only include specifications and drawings explicitly marked confidential. This contradicts the original broader definition in Section 1 and subsequent references to 'Information' throughout the document, because it creates an In-Text Contradiction, the original definition included all types of technical, financial, and business data, which is now excluded unless explicitly marked confidential.
perturbation.contradicted_text: All information of the disclosing party disclosed to or otherwise available to the other party as a result of the discussions hereunder or subsequent work with each other shall be protected hereunder as Proprietary Information of the disclosing party unless: a. if in writing or other tangible form, it is conspicuously labeled by the disclosing party as not Proprietary Information; and b. if oral, it is identified by the disclosing party as not proprietary Information.
perturbation.justification: The changed text significantly narrows the definition of "Information" to only include specifications and drawings explicitly marked as confidential. This directly contradicts the contradicted text, which broadly protects all information disclosed or available as a result of discussions or work, unless explicitly labeled as non-proprietary. The change introduces a clear and direct conflict, as the contradicted section assumes a broader definition of 'Information' that is no longer valid under the modified definition, creating an In-Text Contradiction

perturbation.original_text: Proprietary Information may not be reproduced or copied, in part or in whole, without the prior written consent of the disclosing party.
perturbation.changed_text: Proprietary Information may be reproduced or copied freely without the prior written consent of the disclosing party.
perturbation.explanation: This perturbation directly contradicts the original text of Section 5, removing the need for consent to copy Proprietary Information. This contradiction creates uncertainty about the protection afforded to Proprietary Information under the agreement, specifically in the enforcement of rights regarding its reproduction.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Except as otherwise specified and subject to the provisions of Paragraph 6 below with respect to any Proprietary Information provided hereunder, the receiving party shall use the highest degree of care and discretion to limit disclosure of such Proprietary Information including taking steps: a. to restrict disclosure of Proprietary Information solely to its employees with a need to know and not disclose such Proprietary Information to any other parties; b. to advise all employees and Authorized Advisors of receiving party with access to the Proprietary Information of the obligation for protecting the Proprietary Information as provided hereunder; and c. to use the Proprietary Information provided hereunder only for purposes directly related to the Purposes described first above herein and for no other purposes. The "Authorized Advisor" is such other person(s), who is an advisor to receiving party necessary for the Purposes described first above, who disclosing party in writing has authorized to receive Proprietary Information of disclosing party, and who agrees in writing to the satisfaction of disclosing party to be bound by the terms hereof. Proprietary Information may not be reproduced or copied, in part or in whole, without the prior written consent of the disclosing party.
perturbation.justification: The original text in the contradicted section states that 'Proprietary Information may not be reproduced or copied, in part or in whole, without the prior written consent of the disclosing party.' The changed text states that 'Proprietary Information may be reproduced or copied freely without the prior written consent of the disclosing party.' This directly contradicts the restriction on reproduction and copying established in the contradicted text, creating a clear in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: All Proprietary Information (including any reproductions and copies thereof) shall remain the property of the disclosing party and shall be returned by the receiving party to the disclosing party upon request.
perturbation.changed_text: All Proprietary Information (including any reproductions and copies thereof) becomes the property of the receiving party immediately upon disclosure and does not need to be returned to the disclosing party.
perturbation.explanation: This change directly contradicts the ownership clause of Section 6. Originally, Proprietary Information remained the property of the disclosing party and had to be returned upon request. Now, it becomes the property of the receiving party and does not need to be returned, creating a direct contradiction regarding ownership and return obligations and a In-Text Contradiction.
perturbation.contradicted_text: All Proprietary Information (including any reproductions and copies thereof) shall remain the property of the disclosing party and shall be returned by the receiving party to the disclosing party upon request. No disclosure of any Proprietary Information hereunder shall be construed a public disclosure of such Proprietary Information by either party for any purpose whatever. The obligations imposed upon either party herein shall not apply to Information whether or not designated as Proprietary Information which is disclosed pursuant to a valid order of a court or other governmental <PAGE> body or any political subdivision thereof; provided, however, that the recipient of the order shall first have given notice to the disclosing party and made a reasonable effort to obtain a protective order requiring that the Information and/or documents so disclosed to be used only for the purposes for which the order was issued.
perturbation.justification: The changed text states that proprietary information becomes the property of the receiving party upon disclosure and does not need to be returned. The contradicted text states that proprietary information remains the property of the disclosing party and shall be returned. This is a direct and clear contradiction regarding ownership and return obligations, as described in the Perturbation Explanation and aligns with the definition of Structural Flaws - In Text Contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: ADC and Polaroid hereby agree not to circumvent, or to attempt to circumvent, this Agreement in an effort to deprive the other party to this agreement of fees, commissions or other remuneration, in connection with the use of Confidential Information and/or in pursuit of the above listed purposes and projects, and both parties shall indemnify the other against any circumvention or attempt to circumvent by the offending party.
perturbation.changed_text: ADC and Polaroid are permitted to circumvent this Agreement in an effort to deprive the other party to this agreement of fees, commissions or other remuneration, in connection with the use of Confidential Information and/or in pursuit of the above listed purposes and projects, and neither party is required to indemnify the other against any circumvention or attempt to circumvent.
perturbation.explanation: This change is the direct opposite of Section 9. Previously, circumvention was prohibited and subject to indemnification. Now, it is permitted, and indemnification is not required. This creates a serious contradiction regarding the parties' obligations to act in good faith. A in-text contradiction is created when the receiving party deprives the disclosing party of fees or other remuneration.
perturbation.contradicted_text: ADC and Polaroid hereby agree not to circumvent, or to attempt to circumvent, this Agreement in an effort to deprive the other party to this agreement of fees, commissions or other remuneration, in connection with the use of Confidential Information and/or in pursuit of the above listed purposes and projects, and both parties shall indemnify the other against any circumvention or attempt to circumvent by the offending party.
perturbation.justification: The changed text directly contradicts the contradicted text. The original text explicitly prohibits circumvention and requires indemnification, while the changed text explicitly permits circumvention and waives indemnification. This creates a direct conflict within the document regarding the parties' rights and obligations, thus there is a strong contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: In the event either party discloses, disseminates or releases any Proprietary Information received from the other party or threatens to do so, except as authorized hereunder, such disclosure, dissemination or release will be deemed a material breach of this Agreement and the disclosing party may demand prompt return of all Proprietary Information previously provided to such party and obtain a preliminary and permanent injunction enjoining any such disclosure, dissemination or release.
perturbation.changed_text: In the event either party discloses, disseminates or releases any Proprietary Information received from the other party or threatens to do so, such disclosure, dissemination or release will not be deemed a breach of this Agreement, and the disclosing party has no right to demand the return of Proprietary Information or to obtain an injunction.
perturbation.explanation: This change completely negates the remedies available to a party whose Proprietary Information is disclosed without authorization, directly contradicting the original clause in Section 11. It removes the material breach designation, the right to demand the return of Proprietary Information, and the ability to obtain an injunction. Thus an In-Text contradiction is being made, because the original definition afforded protections not included in the current.
perturbation.contradicted_text: In the event either party discloses, disseminates or releases any Proprietary Information received from the other party or threatens to do so, except as authorized hereunder, such disclosure, dissemination or release will be deemed a material breach of this Agreement and the disclosing party may demand prompt return of all Proprietary Information previously provided to such party and obtain a preliminary and permanent injunction enjoining any such disclosure, dissemination or release. The provisions of this paragraph are in addition to any other legal right or remedies the party whose Proprietary Information has been disclosed, disseminated or released may have under federal or state law including without limitation, any claims for disclosing party's direct and consequential damages. The prevailing party in any dispute hereunder shall be entitled to its costs and expenses in connection with enforcement and any claim for damages for any violation of this Agreement, including its reasonable attorneys' fees and court costs.
perturbation.justification: The changed text states that disclosing Proprietary Information will 'not be deemed a breach of this Agreement' and the disclosing party has no right to remedies, directly contradicting the contradicted text, which states that such disclosure 'will be deemed a material breach of this Agreement' and provides remedies such as demanding the return of Proprietary Information and obtaining an injunction. The change negates the protections afforded by the original text, creating a strong in-text contradiction as defined by the prompt.

perturbation.original_text: This agreement shall be effective, covering all present and future negotiations by and between ADC and Polaroid concerning the use by ADC and Polaroid of such Confidential Information and/or projects from the date of this Agreement and shall continue for three (3) years. If ADC and Polaroid enter into any collateral agreements during this three year period, the provisions of this Non-disclosure and Non-circumvent agreement shall remain effective and in force until the expiration of any such subsequent or collateral agreements.
perturbation.changed_text: This agreement is effective immediately and terminates automatically 30 days from the effective date, regardless of ongoing negotiations or collateral agreements.
perturbation.explanation: The original text in Section 13 specifies a term of three years, potentially extended by collateral agreements. The modification drastically shortens the agreement to 30 days, creating a direct contradiction regarding the duration of the agreement, because this clause specifies a shorter time for confidentiality, generating an In-Text Contradiction.
perturbation.contradicted_text: This agreement shall be effective, covering all present and future negotiations by and between ADC and Polaroid concerning the use by ADC and Polaroid of such Confidential Information and/or projects from the date of this Agreement and shall continue for three (3) years. If ADC and Polaroid enter into any collateral agreements during this three year period, the provisions of this Non-disclosure and Non-circumvent agreement shall remain effective and in force until the expiration of any such subsequent or collateral agreements.
perturbation.justification: The original text and the contradicted text both state that the agreement lasts for three years, potentially extended by collateral agreements. The changed text shortens the duration to 30 days, directly contradicting the explicitly stated three-year term in the contradicted text. This creates a clear and strong contradiction regarding the agreement's duration, making enforcement uncertain. Therefore, the answer is YES.
